At its most recent meeting on Jul-y 8, the Fair Political
practices comnission undertook a reevaluation of its regulation
2 Cal. Adn. Code Section 18700(a) defining "member" of "a decision-
making body. " There was strong sentiment among some Commission
membeis that subsection (a) (I) ic) of the regulation was resulting
in the unwarranted inclusion of advisory board members within
conflict of interest codes. The matter will be furthe:: discussed
at meetings scheduled for July 20 and early Augus!: .1!. that time'
there is iubstantial likelihood that Section 18700(a) (I) (C) will
be eliminated.
In light of these develoPments, f will not attempt to
render finil

officials', by virtue of 2 Cal . Adm. Code Section 18700(a) (1) (C),

Attention: 
Yir . I,lichae1 Bennet.t
Executive Director
Dear Mr. Bennett:
This Board is required by ti-re Statutes of 1976, Chapter
176, commonly referred to as the Timber Yield Tax lraw, to appoint
a "timber advisory committee". Please provide us with an official
ruling as to vrhether or not the memlcers of this standing comnittee
are subject to the "Political 
Reform Act of 1974".
Pursuant to Section 431 (c) of the Revenue and Taxation
Code the cornmittee is to be composed of one member of this Board,
one representative of the Board of Forestry, five county assessors,
one member representing sma1I-sca1e timber owners, and one rnember
represent.ing large-scaIe timber owners. The Board is required by
Sections 434 through 434.5 of the Revenue Coale to consult with the
comnittee prior to preparing temporary, and eventually permanent
rul-es setting forth criteria and procedures for grading iimberland
on the basis of its site quality and operability. 
The criteria 
and
procedures are to be used by all assessors to graale timberland for
tax purposes. The Board must additionally, following consultation
with the committee, adopt rules containing schedules of values which
the assessor must use as the value of property graded pursuant to
Boardrs site classification rules.
Although the eLected and state employee members of the
committee have responsibilities under the Political Reform Act, we
are uncertain concerning their responsibil ities as members and, more
particularly, the appropriate interpretation of sections 18700 and
18703 of Article I of chapter 7 of Title 2 in Division VI of the
California Administrative Code.
since the 1aw requires
future, we would appreciate your
decision could have substantial
industry rePresentatives . 
Thank
us to take action in the very near
giving this matter priority. 
Your
impact on our ability to obtain
you in advance for your assistance.
Very truly yours ,

sections.conclusion: In light of these developments, I will not attempt to render final advice until the Commission's early August meeting. In the interim, my advice is that even if Timber Advisory Committee members are, for the time being, to be deemed "public officials" by virtue of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1)(C), their participation in matters which will have a general effect on the timber industry, or the subgroups of that industry which you represent, is permitted by virtue of the Commission's regulation defining "effect on the public generally," 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18703(d).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the Timber Advisory Committee established under the Timber Yield Tax Law are subject to the Political Reform Act of 1974, and how sections 18700 and 18703 of the California Administrative Code apply to their participation in committee decisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC will provide final advice after its August meeting, but interim guidance permits Timber Advisory Committee members to participate in matters affecting the timber industry generally under the "effect on the public generally" exception, even if they are deemed public officials.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some formatting issues; contains both the incoming request (June 10, 1976) and FPPC response letter (July 16, 1976); FPPC was reconsidering Section 18700(a)(1)(C) regarding advisory board member definitions at the time of response.

CONCLUSION: In light of these developments, I will not attempt to render final advice until the Commission's early August meeting. In the interim, my advice is that even if Timber Advisory Committee members are, for the time being, to be deemed "public officials" by virtue of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1)(C), their participation in matters which will have a general effect on the timber industry, or the subgroups of that industry which you represent, is permitted by virtue of the Commission's regulation defining "effect on the public generally," 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18703(d).

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the State Board of Equalization's inquiry about whether Timber Advisory Committee members are subject to the Political Reform Act, providing interim advice that permits their participation in industry matters affecting the public generally while deferring final guidance pending a regulatory reevaluation scheduled for August.
